Video.summary: Time-saving quick tips to feed your crafting craving.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Learn to sew, step-by-step with National Sewing Circle experts on CraftsyTV.
Video.grandparentTitle: National Sewing Circle presented by CraftsyTV
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Welcome to Artists's Academy where we are dedicated to making you a better artist. We believe everyone has an artist within, waiting to be unleashed. Our mission is to be your ultimate resource for visual art education using tutorials covering everything from fundamental techniques to advanced concepts in painting and drawing.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Learn to knit, step-by-step with The Knitting Circle experts on CraftsyTV.
Video.grandparentTitle: The Knitting Circle Presented by CraftsyTV
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: We're having trouble seeing straight. Then again, it's probably the Drunkard's Path block! Angela's here to make sure the steps go "straight to your head."
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Make technology your friend. It's hard to keep up with each and every new trend but you can find your favorites and use them to your advantage. We begin with an awesome page, then it's a project life page with Nancy Nally using instagram photos, and finally a family heirloom with modern techniques but historic info from Paula Nelson-Hart. Great for young family members to get to know their heritage and for older family members to remember and reflect.
Video.title: Keeping Up

Video.summary: Creative Crochet Corner is an online video streaming service all about learning how to crochet! Whether you're a new to crochet just learning the basics or an experienced crocheter looking for a challenge, Creative Crochet Corner is here to help. We offer a vast library of video tutorials taught by talented crochet experts who guide you through each technique step-by-step, sharing their best tips and tricks.
Video.grandparentTitle: Creative Crochet Corner presented by CraftsyTV
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: A dull tool is a dangerous tool when it comes to woodworking. George Vondriska shares his top tips for sharpening your woodworking tools so you can ensure the cleanest cuts and get the most out of your blades. From lathe chisels to router bits, George explains the steps you'll need to take to keep your tools as sharp as your skills in part two of this two-part lesson.
Video.grandparentTitle: Woodworkers Guild of America presented by CraftsyTV
Video.title: Sharpening Your Woodworking Tools Part 2

Video.summary: There are many sources for inspiration when choosing a sewing project. Sometimes we find the perfect pattern or silhouette, and sometimes it's a special occasion we want to sew for, other times we simply love a particular fabric and look for something to make out of a gorgeous cut. But each fabric has characteristics that require different techniques. We chose 3 fabrics today and projects that are unique to the fabric chosen. First, Angela shows her technique for needle felting on silk. Next, Tina Givens introduces us to one of her favorite fabrics -- linen. She has some tips for quilt blocks that tell a unique story. Then, Angela returns for free motion techniques on cotton.
Video.title: Fabric First

Video.summary: An artist sees things differently -- they have a unique way of looking at the world and interpreting it into art. First, we look at Mary Cassatt, an American painter and printmaker known for her portrayal of women and children and create a project showing the bonds between mother and child. Using a totally different perspective, Joe Rotella, creates a kaleidoscope guaranteed to alter the way you see the world. We finish up with Ardith Goodwin and a Kandinsky inspired work -- starting with a photo of a house and using chalk and neon to create her canvas.
Video.title: In the Eyes of an Artist

Image.alt: In the Eyes of an Artist
In the Eyes of an Artist
In the Eyes of an Artist

Video.summary: Renovate the surface of your pages. Julie Fei-Fan Balzer adds depth to her designs with amazing shrinking embellishments. May Flaum creates dynamic designs with misty effects using scraps. Ashley Horton has a tip for storing tiny letters and embellishments. Cheryl Boglioli makes 3-D designs with fringed fabric and felt.
Video.title: Episode 6: DIY Embellishments

Video.summary: Today's show is all about texture -- texture is the feel or shape of a surface or thing. It's the smoothness or roughness, or maybe how soft or hard. We will learn about texture with a Paleolithic cave painting, a fun pony corral, and a friendly but prickly hedgehog. Learn how to add texture to your drawings with ordinary things from around the house in the Art Journal segment. Last, make a furry friend with giant chenille stems.
Video.title: Texture

Video.summary: One of the hallmarks of modern quilting is the creative use of negative space. Audrey Esarey shares her thoughts on how to use negative space to highlight motifs. Then, Ebony Love has a lesson on partial seams – learn this technique and you will be surprised just how much you use it. Learn about chain piecing in the machine tip with Eliane Bergmann. You can chain piece an entire quilt!
Video.title: Negative Space

Video.summary: Quilting just got better. Join Angela in her first episode as she stitches up her exclusive variable-star quilt design.
Video.title: Stars

Video.summary: Angela loves to stock up on fabrics. From bundles to collections, get her tips to pick the perfect prints and solids for your next quilt.
Video.title: Bonus: How to Choose Fabric

Video.summary: Angela’s not the only Midnight Quilter in the Walters' house! Watch as she and her teenage daughter Cloe spend some quality time together making an easy-to-piece charm quilt.
Video.title: Like a Charm

Video.summary: Angela's been challenged by The Crafty Gemini to make a stash-busting tote bag. So she's stepping outside her comfort zone and up to the #MidnightStashChallenge!
Video.title: Quilted Tote Bag Challenge

Video.summary: It was only a matter of time before the Midnight Quilt Show tackled Janice Ryan's Wallflower Quilt! You bring the wine, Angela's got the Flying Geese covered.
Video.title: Wallflower Variable Star Quilt

Video.summary: Picking out your first quilting pattern can feel a bit overwhelming — but Angela is here to help! Learn how big blocks, straight lines and no matching points can make all the difference for beginner quilters.
Video.title: Bonus: Easy Quilt Patterns for Beginners

Image.alt: Bonus: Easy Quilt Patterns for Beginners
Bonus: Easy Quilt Patterns for Beginners
Bonus: Easy Quilt Patterns for Beginners

Video.summary: She's Baaaaaack! Been itchin' for wine + stitchin'? Angela's kicking off a brand new season with a rather "puzzling" episode.
Video.title: Puzzle

Video.summary: Are you Quilt Curious, but unsure where to get started? THIS is the Midnight Quilt Show episode for you!
Video.title: Friendship Stars Quilt

Video.summary: Believe it or not, this quilt is only made of straight seams! Watch it "unfold" as Angela conquers the Strip City pattern with Boundless blues.
Video.title: Strip City

Video.summary: Angela shares her top three tips on how to cut t-shirts for a quilt.
Video.title: Bonus: How to Cut T-Shirts for a Quilt

Image.alt: Bonus: How to Cut T-Shirts for a Quilt
Bonus: How to Cut T-Shirts for a Quilt
Bonus: How to Cut T-Shirts for a Quilt

Video.summary: Angela is kicking off Season 7 of the Midnight Quilt Show with a redemption story. She’s made this Thistle quilt pattern before with half-square triangles but thinks this flying-geese version will be much easier to quilt.
Video.title: Flying Geese Quilt Redo!

Video.summary: Ribbons and trims have been around forever, but it's only recently that we've seen so many new ways to actually use them as part of garment construction. Today's experts use ribbons and trims as an integral part of their design. Joanne Banko shows how to embellish a dress with decoratively stitched bands you make yourself. Then, Angela Wolf uses bias tape trim on the neckline of the continuing Little Black Dress lesson. Last is Sunni Standing and a waistband for a pencil skirt created from ribbon.
Video.title: Classic Use of Ribbons and Trims

Image.alt: Classic Use of Ribbons and Trims
Classic Use of Ribbons and Trims
Classic Use of Ribbons and Trims

Video.summary: Metal comes in so many shapes and forms. Explore two very different types on this episode. First, Leslie Rogalski creates a centerline bracelet, the actual bracelet is pre-shaped chevron metal with holes and and an opening to string seed beads. Then, Tammy Honaman has another metal clay lesson.
Video.title: Metal Forms

Video.summary: Artist Catherine Bickford presents ideas for meaningful gifts, including the symbolism of color and design motifs. Catherine discusses the meaning and symbolism behind various design motifs and materials. She shows how to combine themes, materials, techniques and motifs to create a talisman necklace that has significance and well-wishes for the recipient. Fair trade beaded bangles are featured on Katie's Beading Lesson.
Video.title: Meaningful Gifts and Talismans

Video.summary: Connect your designs in new ways. Warm up as host Katie Hacker designs with colorful stones. She adds beads and metal spacers to enhance the stone elements in necklaces. Author Lisa Crone works out with memory wire to create a sparkling necklace. Then, she cools down by making matching earrings.
Video.title: Get Connected to Fitness

Video.summary: Combine materials, mediums and techniques to create a collage. Julie Fei Fan Balzer has a collage project using layers of stencils. Urban artist Seth Apter die cuts components for an industrial-looking collage. Mixed media artist Rebekah Meier paints, stamps and sews a colorful cloth banner. Jenn Mason has a tip for capturing images while you travel.
Video.title: Collage

Video.summary: Learn to bake and cook with CraftsyTV.
Video.title: Baking & Cooking

Video.summary: Did you know there is more than one way to do a yarn over? Yup, there are actually four! This week Tracey and Jodi demonstrate all four techniques and talk about when to use each.
Video.title: Four Ways to Yarn Over in Pattern & Lace Knitting Demystified

Image.alt: Four Ways to Yarn Over in Pattern & Lace Knitting Demystified
Four Ways to Yarn Over in Pattern & Lace Knitting Demystified
Four Ways to Yarn Over in Pattern & Lace Knitting Demystified

Video.summary: The ladies are going big by thinking small! From compact cables to cute mini skeins, this one's all about the tiny treasures that keep those needles clicking.
Video.title: Cutest Projects Ever: Small Knitting, Big Impact

Image.alt: Cutest Projects Ever: Small Knitting, Big Impact
Cutest Projects Ever: Small Knitting, Big Impact
Cutest Projects Ever: Small Knitting, Big Impact

Video.summary: When creative ideas pop up, write them down. Make a colorful pocket journal with Jenny Barnett-Rohrs. Candie Cooper shares creative tips for handwriting and Franz Spohn writes with clay. Katie Hacker decorates for fall with a shining pumpkin luminary from blogger Carissa Bonham.
Video.title: Write It Down

Video.summary: Welcome to CraftsyTV's Cook & Create, where delectable delights meet crafty creators.
Video.title: Cook & Create

Video.summary: Learn to quilt step by step with National Quilting Circle experts on CraftsyTV.
Video.title: National Quilting Circle presented by CraftsyTV

Image.alt: National Quilting Circle presented by CraftsyTV
National Quilting Circle presented by CraftsyTV
National Quilting Circle presented by CraftsyTV
